data.title: Disparity is on the menu: Restaurant stocks deliver a wide mix of returns
data.description:
Unlike many industries where stocks tend to move in the same general direction, the restaurant sector has proven far more fragmented in 2025. Performance among food and beverage names has varied widely, with some companies delivering outsized gains while others have suffered steep declines.

For example, Potbelly (PBPB) has surged more than 80% year-to-date, standing out as one of the strongest performers in the space. By contrast, Sweetgreen (SG) has collapsed by nearly 75%, underscoring the uneven landscape investors face when navigating this group. 

This divergence highlights the importance of stock-specific fundamentals, as broader industry momentum has offered little predictive value. 

Below is a breakdown of 32 restaurant stocks that illustrates the striking disparity in performance across the sector. 

No. 1: Potbelly (PBPB) +81% YTD.

No. 2: Nathan’s Famous (NATH) +41.2% YTD. 

No. 3: Biglari (BH) +27% YTD. 

No. 4: Papa John’s (PZZA) +17.4% YTD.

No. 5: Cheesecake Factory (CAKE) +15.2% YTD.

No. 6: Yum! Brands (YUM) +14.1% YTD.

No. 7: McDonald’s (MCD) +36% YTD.

No. 8: Darden Restaurants (DRI) +3% YTD.

No. 9: Aramark (ARMK) +2.7% YTD.

No. 10: Domino’s Pizza (DPZ) +2% YTD.

No. 11: Dutch Bros (BROS) -1.6% YTD.

No. 12: Brinker Intl (EAT) -5.4% YTD.

No. 13: Starbucks (SBUX) -7.5% YTD.

No. 14: Wingstop (WING) -7.6% YTD.

No. 15: Texas Roadhouse (TXRH) -9.7% YD.

No. 16: DineEquity (DIN) -11.5% YTD.

No. 17: BJ’s Restaurants (BJRI) -13% YTD.

No. 18: Denny’s (DENN) -14.3% YTD.

No. 19: First Watch Restaurant (FWRG) -15.5% YTD.

No. 20: Cracker Barrell (CRBL) -17.9% YTD. 

No. 21: El Pollo Loco (LOCO) -18.2% YTD.

No. 22: Shake Shack (SHAK) -29.4% YTD.

No. 23: Portillos (PLTO) -31.4% YTD. 

No. 24: Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) -35.3% YTD.

No. 25: Kura Sushi (KRUS) -35.6% YTD.

No. 26: Dave & Buster’s (PLAY) -38.2% YTD.

No. 27: Bloomin Brands (BLMN) -41.7% YTD.

No. 28: Wendy’s (WEN) -41.9% YTD.

For more on the restaurant and food & beverage space see below a group of ETFs:

data.title: U.S. looks to ease UK whisky imports, lowering risk of retaliatory tariffs on U.S. counterparts - report
data.description:
Washington is weighing tariff relief on imported UK whisky, a step that would remove the threat of retaliatory tariffs on U.S. bourbon while at the same time benefit American distillers that supply used oak barrels to UK producers.

According to Bloomberg, U.S. officials have discussed the possibility to carve out tariff exemptions for UK whisky producers, currently subject to a 10% tax to import their whisky into the U.S.

According to data out of the Scotch Whisky Association, the trade barrier costs UK distillers £20M per month ($26.9M) in lost exports, while 60% of all UK scotch is aged in barrels from Kentucky.

Although the U.S. and UK reached a trade agreement in May to exempt certain UK industries, the UK whisky business is still subject to a 10% tax, leaving domestic producers like Brown-Foreman (BF.A) (NYSE:BF.B), Diageo (DEO) -- which makes rum in the U.S. Virgin Islands -- and MGP Ingredients (MGPI) vulnerable to retaliatory tariffs.

Additionally, because American law requires all bourbon to be aged in new oak barrels, a majority of the used barrels are sold to foreign counterparts to be used again (some barrels are reused to age scotch, rum, tequila, and craft beer), opening up another revenue source for U.S. industries.

data.title: Yahoo in talks to sell AOL as owner Apollo eyes $1.4B deal with Italian tech firm
data.description:
Apollo Global-owned company Yahoo (NYSE:APO) is in advanced talks to sell AOL to Italy's Bending Spoons for about $1.4B, Reuters reported late on Wednesday, citing four sources.

The Milan-based technology company is in advanced talks to purchase the legacy media brand from the U.S.-based asset manager, but sources told the news agency that a final agreement has not been signed and talks could still fall apart.

The deal would add AOL's vast user base to Bending Spoons' portfolio of mobile applications, the report said.

AOL, which makes money from advertising and through its subscription services, including LifeLock identity theft protection, LastPass password management, and McAfee Multi Access malware protection, recently saw its website traffic grow 20% from last year among the users aged 25 and 54, outpacing the growth in the category of users aged 55-plus, a source familiar with AOL's performance told Reuters.

The growth was driven by the introduction of multiple new content categories to AOL.com, including Health, Fitness, Animals, Science & Tech, Home & Garden, Lighter Side, True Crime, and Local, amongst others, the source told Reuters.

Bending Spoons purchases struggling tech firms and revamps them. The company has 300M monthly users across its products and has done several acquisitions recently. Last month it agreed to a deal to take private video platform company Vimeo for $1.38B, which is its largest acquisition to date.

data.title: SoFi starts offering no-fee options trading for beginners
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (NASDAQ:SOFI) started rolling out no-fee options products for retail investors who are new to the market, the company said Thursday. It will be available to all eligible SoFi Invest members in coming weeks.

Members who are approved will gain access for so-called level 1 trading, including covered calls and cash-secured puts, in addition to the options level 2 strategies already available. The aim is to help investors better manage their portfolio, generate premium income, enter new positions, and hedge existing ones to manage risk, SoFi (NASDAQ:SOFI) said.

"One of the most consistent requests we heard from SoFi Invest members was access to Options Level 1, and we’re thrilled to deliver on that demand," said Anthony Noto, CEO of SoFi (NASDAQ:SOFI). "By expanding access to options trading and embedding education directly into the experience, members can responsibly diversify their strategies and pursue their long-term financial goals with confidence, all within SoFi’s one-stop shop."

The company is removing fees associated with options exercise and assignment in an effort to make options trading smoother and more transparent and to reduce costs for active options traders, it said.

Eligibility is determined by factors such as trading experience, investment objectives, and financial situation.

SoFi (NASDAQ:SOFI) also introduced alternative funds from ARK, KKR (KKR), Carlyle Group (CG), and Franklin-Templeton (BEN), covering private credit, real estate, and pre-IPO firm. In addition, it relaunched its robo-advisor in partnership with BlackRock (BLK), which also includes portfolios that invest in alternative assets.

Note that the company warned that "options trading involves significant risk and is not suitable for all investors."

SoFi (SOFI) stock slipped 0.4% in Thursday morning trading.

data.title: INCY and ABBV lead S&P 500 healthcare gainers in Q3; MOH and ALGN lag
data.description:
The Health Care Select Sector SPDR® Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLV), which tracks the health care sector, and holds a weightage of 12.12% in the S&P 500 index, advanced 1.80% in Q3, falling short of the S&P 500's 7.91% rise. Year-to-date, the performance gap is wider: the XLV has gained only 4.29%, trailing the S&P 500's robust 14.10% increase.

As the Q3 earnings season begins, investors are scrutinizing the healthcare sector's outlook. The XLV ETF fares well on several key metrics, earning an A+ score for dividends, expenses, and liquidity. However, the ETF's current performance is undermined by poor market mechanics, reflected in its low ratings of D for risk/volatility and D+ for momentum.

The sector includes major corporations across pharmaceuticals, healthcare services, biotech, and medical equipment. Its largest constituents include Eli Lilly + Co (NYSE:LLY), UnitedHealth Group Inc (NYSE:UNH), and Johnson + Johnson (NYSE:JNJ).

IQVIA (NYSE:IQV) +16.81%

Bottom 5 S&P 500 healthcare performers in Q3:

Molina Healthcare (NYSE:MOH) -37.52%

Align Technology (NASDAQ:ALGN) -35.55%

Baxter (NYSE:BAX) -27.09%

DexCom (NASDAQ:DXCM) -19.83%

Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV) -18.35%

What Quantitative Measures Say

According to Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system, the healthcare sector has a score of 3.18 out of 5 with a Neutral rating. The system awards grades based on quantitative measures, like valuation, earnings growth, and recent stock performance. The highest possible score for any individual company is 5.

The index fares well on profitability metrics with a score of A, but it scores a D+ on growth and C+ on valuation and C in momentum.

McKesson (NYSE:MCK) has the highest score of 4.82 with a Strong Buy rating as the company achieved record Q2 earnings, with three segments delivering double-digit growth, reflecting strong momentum across the entire business and raised its fiscal 2026 outlook ahead of the consensus in conjunction with its 2025 Investor Day, followed by Boston Scientific Corporation (NYSE:BSX) rating of 4.68 and Incyte Corporation (NASDAQ:INCY) with a quant rating of 4.66.

The lowest quant-rated stocks coincide with the worst performers for the Q3— Bio-Techne (NASDAQ:TECH), Align Technology (NASDAQ:ALGN) has a Strong Sell rating with a score of 1.28, and 1.30, respectively, and the lowest factor grades given to growth.

Industries Q3 performance

The Health Care Equipment & Services sector rose 2.48% in Q3, while Pharmaceuticals, Biotechnology and Life Sciences rose 5.21% in Q3.

In healthcare this week

In light of Pfizer's (NYSE:PFE) announcement on Tuesday that it will offer its drugs at reduced prices to American consumers, President Trump is backing off his threat to impose 100% tariffs on imported pharmaceuticals to give other drugmakers time to cut their own deals. As part of the agreement announced on Tuesday, Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) has agreed to provide four of its brand-name drugs at sharp discounts to cash-paying customers through a government-run website called TrumpRx and to Medicare. The company also plans a $70B U. S. investment in exchange for tariff relief.

Recently, Pfizer (PFE) has made a $4.9 billion deal to acquire Metsera, aiming to enter the obesity treatment market currently led by Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk.

On Tuesday, Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has also announced that it is in talks with the Trump administration to provide discounted drugs to consumers as part of the new TrumpRx direct-to-consumer website.

Related ETFs: Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLV), SPDR S&P Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:XPH), VanEck Pharmaceutical ETF (NASDAQ:PPH), iShares Nasdaq Biotechnology ETF (NASDAQ:IBB), iShares U.S. Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:IHE), and Invesco Nasdaq Biotechnology ETF (NASDAQ:IBBQ).

More on The Health Care Select Sector SPDR® Fund ETF

  • XLV: Market Overreaction Could Drive Double Digit Shareholder Yield
  • XLV Vs. IHE: Will Pharmaceuticals Outperform Healthcare?
  • XLV: Life Expectancy, Patents, And A Positive Outlook Will Pave The Way
  • Notable healthcare headlines for the week: Novo Nordisk and UnitedHealth in focus
  • SA Asks: How will H-1B visa changes impact the healthcare sector?

data.title: Deutsche Bank’s ‘Conviction list’ for Q4: KO, SCHW, LLY and more
data.description:
Deutsche Bank’s research team selected their top investment ideas to hold over the next 12 months, with new picks including Wells Fargo (WFC), McKesson (MCK), Union Pacific (UNP), and NXP Semiconductors (NXPI), among others.

The bank’s selection of their “Conviction List, Fresh Money” picks has returned 29% on a rolling 12-month basis, compared to the S&P 500’s (SP500) 22% return. Also, since its inception in 2017, these groups have returned an average of 241%, vs. 213% for the S&P 500 (SP500).

Here are the stocks, divided by sector:

Consumer

  • Hyatt Hotels (H) – Price target: $172. “We believe Hyatt has the best catalyst stack in lodging over the next twelve months, leading to multiple expansion and upside to numbers.”
  • The Coca-Cola Company (KO) – Price target: $81. “Acknowledging near-term macro/geopolitical headwinds to volume, we see KO as best positioned among CPG peers with solid earnings visibility into FY26 and improving FCF conversion.”
  • Life Time Group (LTH) – Price target: $36. “We believe LTH has a unique blend of organic and external growth drivers that are not overly reliant on favorable macroeconomic conditions.”
  • Starbucks (SBUX) – Price target: $107. “We see SBUX as among the best multi-year growth stories in restaurants, with significant upside to medium- and long-term numbers (both sales and margins).”

Financials & Fintech

  • American International Group (AIG) – Price target: $91. “We believe AIG will likely benefit from renewed capital markets activity, driving premium growth in its flagship financial lines business.”
  • Charles Schwab (SCHW) – Price target: $113. “SCHW offers a solid risk/return profile with strong double-digit EPS growth from an increasingly diversified revenue base, improving organic growth and a strengthened NII outlook within context of like Fed cuts.”
  • S&P Global (SPGI) – Price target: $622. “We view SPGI as a diversified multi-year compounder with earnings upside from market appreciation and normalization in capital markets over time.”
  • Synchrony Financial (SYF) – Price target: $91. “We continue to believe SYF is positioned to outperform as the launch of its new Walmart partnership reaccelerates loan growth without the need to compromise on underwriting standards.”
  • Webster Financial (WBS) – Price target: $73. “We continue to believe WBS shares are positioned to play both offense and defense and set up to navigate different macro scenarios given strong capital levels, solid credit, and above peer loan growth.”
  • Welltower, Inc. (WELL) – Price target: $195. “WELL is positioned to deliver outsized performance through disciplined execution, strategic acquisitions, and supportive supply-demand trends in senior housing.”
  • Wells Fargo (WFC) – Price target: $100. “We are optimistic WFC could re-rate higher given the potential for accelerated growth driven by strategic investments that WFC has made over time, despite recent underperformance.”

Healthcare

  • Acadia Healthcare (ACHC) – Price target: $36. “We expect ACHC to outperform over the NTM (next 12 months) as an activist provides a clear pathway to reducing ACHC’s steep discount to the intrinsic value of its assets.”
  • Alkermes PLC (ALKS) – Price target: $53. “Despite recent weakness, we would remain buyers of ALKS heading into NT2 (narcolepsy type 2) data, as we believe alixorexton safety concerns are overdone, and efficacy is above peers.”
  • Danaher (DHR) – Price target: $235. “Danaher’s portfolio revamp and DBS-driven execution position it for faster core growth, backed by world-class leadership and a strong balance sheet trading at (20%) discount to S&P versus historical 30-40% premium.”
  • Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) – Price target: $900. “Still the diabesity leader; multiple upcoming readouts (e.g. Retatrutide, Eloralintide), with Orforglipron’s must-see launch on deck.”
  • McKesson (MCK) – Price target: $856. “We believe MCK will continue to outperform this year given the scaled platforms and growth momentum in specialty and oncology. The separation of Medical segment should unlock further value.”
  • Penumbra (PEN) – Price target: $326. “We expect PEN to outperform over the NTM as a slew of influential RCT (randomized controlled trial) readouts in the space support guideline updates that drive mechanical thrombectomy as a front-line therapy for VTE (venous thromboembolism) and drive further adoption.”
  • Repligen (RGEN) – Price target: $180. “Mid-teens IRR (internal rate of return) with 30-40% IRR potential under greater growth and earnings multiple assumptions from mid-teens revenue growth and >1,000 bps of margin expansion through 2030.”

Industrials

  • Archer (ACHR) – Price target: $15. “We believe Archer can bounce back following the recent pullback, supported by progress on its defense initiatives, monetization opportunities in oversea markets, and incremental flight-testing milestones.”
  • Alaska Air Group (ALK) – Price target: $60. “ALK is executing well on its merger with Hawaiian Airlines; the combined company has diversified, resilient revenue streams – i.e., premium, corporate, long-haul international, cargo, loyalty/credit card, etc.”
  • Celanese (CE) – Price target: $52. “We believe Celanese is well-positioned to outperform because of its advantaged acetyls, high-value engineered materials business, intensifying cost reduction actions, and strong deleveraging efforts.”
  • Constellium (CSTM) – Price target: $20. “CSTM offers a valuation discount opportunity for investors with the gap to likely close in the near term, driven by strong execution and favorable tariff environments, despite some sector headwinds.”
  • nVent (NVT) – Price target: $103. “We continue to believe that NVT’s upgraded/higher growth portfolio could produce top-tier organic growth relative to the MI/EE (machinery/industrial electronics) group yielding further margin expansion and the possibility to continue re-rating higher.”
  • Navigator Holdings (NVGS) – Price target: $24. “We believe that fleet utilization will recover into 3Q25 from the recent lows seen in 2Q25 (those were due to trade/tariff uncertainty), which will drive revenues higher to the near-term highs seen in 1Q25.”
  • Visteon Corp. (VC) – Price target: $131. “We think that VC will continue to outperform versus peers as China mix headwind subsides next year, low expectations for BMS (building management systems) priced in, and gain share opportunities with Japanese/Korean OEMs (order management systems) continue to unfold.”
  • Pentair (PNR) – Price target: $122. “Still a long runway for outsized margin expansion driven by cost takeout and 80/20 that could drive DD% (drawdown percentage) estimate revisions; end markets near trough + rate cut winner; “cheap” multiple that should re-rate on execution.”
  • Trex Company (TREX) – Price target: $92. “TREX to outperform on high-single-digit sales growth (2025-2026) and 31% adjusted EBITDA margins, despite weak housing. We also see a path to double-digit top-line growth when residential activity recovers.”
  • Union Pacific (UNP) – Price target: $272. “UNP is the best in-class railroad, levered to an industrial economic recovery with runway for cost efficiency and revenue gains. Additional upside via revenue/cost synergies from the planned transcontinental rail merger.”
  • Woodward (WWD) – Price target: $335. “Woodward’s strong position in the aeroengine value chain should continue to drive double-digit EBITDA and EPS growth despite anxiety around the initial FY26 guide, which we believe will be typically conservative.”

TMT

  • Amazon (AMZN) – Price target: $278. “We continue to believe AMZN remains a top earnings growth story at scale due to its steady double-digit top-line growth backdrop supported by retail/e-commerce share gains and accelerating AI workload growth.”
  • Cellebrite (CLBT) – Price target: $21. “We expect Cellebrite to see outsized benefit from a return in federal spending, given its mission-critical nature and priorities within the new administration.”
  • Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) – Price target: $150. “A more constructive WFE (wafer fab equipment) outlook, coupled with several secular aspects unique to LRCX (e.g., large installed base, leading-edge exposure), should enable the company to outperform its SemiCap peers.”
  • Meta, Inc. (META) – Price target: $930. “We believe META deserves a premium valuation multiple versus peers given near-term tailwinds alongside longer-term strategic advances with ample greenfield opportunities.”
  • NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) – Price target: $265. “We believe this company will continue to outperform despite sector tailwinds because of its diverse growth drivers, consistent execution, and improving structural metrics positioning it for an emerging upcycle.”
  • The New York Times (NYT) – Price target: $68. “We are growing increasingly confident in NYT’s subscriber growth outlook, which remains the biggest debate among investors.”
  • Palo Alto Networks (PANW) – Price target: $235. “We expect PANW to outperform as underlying business momentum remains solid and management executes on a transformational acquisition that positions the company for AI security leadership.”
  • EchoStar (SATS) – Price target: $102. “We believe EchoStar still offers asymmetric reward versus risk given our SOTP (sum of all parts) valuation, which includes announced transactions to sell spectrum for $121/share.”
  • Visa (V) – Price target: $410. “V is positioned to outperform while also providing protection if macro conditions were to deteriorate.”

data.title: Southern California Edison sinks as Trump administration cancels planned upgrade
data.description:
Edison International (NYSE:EIX) -4% in early trading Thursday as Southern California Edison's $600 million federal grant awarded last year to upgrade 100 miles of electric transmission lines will have its funding cut.

The grid upgrade is one of 223 climate-related energy projects in 16 Democrat-led states, including California and New York, valued at more than $3.1 billion that the U.S. Department of Energy said it plans to cancel.

The DoE said the projects "did not adequately advance the nation's energy needs, were not economically viable, and would not provide a positive return on investment of taxpayer dollars," claiming the cancellations would save $7.56 billion.

The planned cancellations reportedly also included funding for proposed hydrogen hubs in California and the Pacific Northwest.

data.title: Anavex rises after trial data for schizophrenia candidate (update)
data.description:
Anavex Life Sciences (NASDAQ:AVXL) traded higher in the premarket on Thursday after the New York-based biotech said that a mid-stage trial for its experimental antipsychotic ANAVEX 3-71 reached the primary endpoint in patients with schizophrenia, a psychiatric illness.

Citing topline data, Anavex (NASDAQ:AVXL) announced that ANAVEX 3-71 was found to be safe and well-tolerated in the placebo-controlled Phase 2 clinical study, which enrolled adults with schizophrenia who were undergoing stable antipsychotic medications.

The company added that ANAVEX 3-71 indicated a tolerability profile consistent with prior trials in healthy volunteers, with no serious treatment-emergent adverse events and no severe TEAEs.

Additionally, citing a secondary and exploratory analysis of the trial, the company stated that favorable trends were observed in several outcome measures, including event-related potential biomarkers of schizophrenia.

“The positive safety profile and encouraging biomarker trends support the continued development of ANAVEX 3-71 as a potential treatment for CNS disorders that could address underlying pathophysiology beyond symptomatic control,” CEO Christopher Missling noted.

In a subsequent release, Anavex (NASDAQ:AVXL) announced the development of a new once-daily oral formulation for the ANAVEX 3-71 program, noting that, in terms of pharmacokinetics, the new modified-release version outperformed the current immediate-release version in a Phase 1b trial.

Update 10:27 AM ET: Adds comments on the new once-daily formulation

data.title: Wall Street hunts for direction on day two of the government shutdown
data.description:
Wall Street looks for direction after an early pop on Thursday that sent both the tech focused Nasdaq Composite and benchmark S&P 500 to new intraday record highs as the government shutdown hit a second day.

For the trading session the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was up by 0.3% and the S&P 500 was near even. At the same time, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was lower by 0.1%. 

From a sector-by-sector vantage point, eight of the 11 S&P segments were in negative trading territory as Real Estate struggled the most so far. At the other end of the spectrum, Info Tech has been the leader on Thursday. 

U.S. Treasury yields inched higher as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved up 1 basis point to 4.11% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed up 2 basis points to 3.56%.

Due to the closure, market participants will be without the usual jobless claims figures. On prediction market Kalshi, the odds of the length of the shutdown ticked up to 11.5 days.

Focus was on the September Challenger job cuts report, which was reported earlier than its scheduled time of 0730 ET. A Challenger spokeswoman told Seeking Alpha that it was a mistake in scheduling and not intentional.

The report showed job cuts fell 37% in September. However, YTD cuts were now the highest since 2020, and hiring the lowest since 2009.

data.title: Taysha soars on FDA breakthrough therapy status for lead asset
data.description:
Taysha Gene Therapies (NASDAQ:TSHA) jumped ~39% on Thursday after the U.S. FDA granted its Breakthrough Therapy designation for the company's lead candidate TSHA-102 as a treatment for Rett syndrome, a rare neurodevelopmental disorder.

Additionally, the gene therapy developer stated that the company, in agreement with the FDA, had finalized a trial protocol and statistical analysis plan for a registrational trial that would support a marketing application for TSHA-102.

“We are pleased to have finalized alignment with the FDA on our pivotal trial protocol and SAP, including the 6-month interim analysis that has the potential to expedite our BLA submission for TSHA-102 by at least two full quarters,” said Taysha’s R&D head, Sukumar Nagendran.

The company reiterated plans to start enrollment for REVEAL in Q4 2025, targeting 15 females with Rett syndrome.

As part of the FDA’s Breakthrough Therapy designation, the company is expected to receive intensive regulatory guidance regarding its R&D plans for TSHA-102 with potential eligibility for the FDA’s priority review.
